dashboard.noPolicyDocsDescription: Policy documents will appear here once attached to a sprint or epic.

dashboard.noProjectAccessDescription: You joined the organization, but you have not been assigned to a project yet. Ask an administrator to assign project access.

memos.surfaceDescription: The operating inbox for project issues, decisions, and requests.
memos.searchMemosPlaceholder: Search by title, content, type, or assignee...
memos.noMatchingMemos: No memos match the current filters.
memos.selectedMemoOutsideView: Selected memo is outside the current view.
memos.detailDescription: Inspect the full memo thread, timeline, and linked docs here.
memos.templateHint: Templates prefill the title, type, and checklist body.
memos.noTeamMember: Could not load team member info. Please complete onboarding.
memos.author: Author

settings.title: Settings
settings.surfaceDescription: Manage notifications, webhooks, invitations, subscriptions, and AI settings from one shared operator surface.
settings.notificationDescription: Toggle web notifications for each operating event.
settings.inviteDescription: Invite teammates and track their status.
settings.dangerDescription: Handle irreversible actions such as account deletion.
settings.subscriptionDescription: Open the subscription portal and manage billing.
settings.deleteAccountDesc: After deleting your account, all personal data will be anonymized after a 30-day grace period. Your content will be preserved.
settings.deleteConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to delete your account?
settings.deleteConfirmDesc: This action cannot be undone. Personal data will be permanently deleted after 30 days.
settings.reauthRequired: Please sign in again before deleting your account.
settings.memberManagementDescription: Review project members and add or remove existing organization members per project.
settings.noAssignableMembers: No organization members available to add
settings.lastProjectMembership: Cannot remove the last active project membership for this member
settings.noProjectMembers: No members assigned to this project
settings.projectManagementDescription: Review every project in this organization and create new ones without leaving Settings.
settings.projectListEmpty: No projects in this organization yet
settings.projectCreated: Project {name} created and added to your workspace.
settings.projectAdminRequired: Only organization admins can create projects from Settings.
settings.projectCreateOrgMissing: Organization context is missing. Refresh and try again.
settings.aiApiKeyUpdatePlaceholder: Enter new key (leave blank to keep existing)
settings.aiApiKeyProviderChangePlaceholder: Enter a new API key for the selected provider
settings.aiApiKeyProviderChangeRequired: Switching providers requires a new API key.
settings.aiBaseUrlPlaceholder: Leave blank to use the default API endpoint

slackIntegration.title: Slack integration & channel mapping
slackIntegration.description: Connect Slack once, review workspace health, and map channels to the right Sprintable project without leaving Settings.
slackIntegration.workspaceConnectedSummary: Workspace ID {teamId} is ready for channel mapping.
slackIntegration.workspaceDisconnectedSummary: Start Slack OAuth to load live channels and map them to projects.
slackIntegration.actionPanelDescription: Pick the right project for each Slack channel, then save or remap with a single confirmation flow.
slackIntegration.helperText: Only organization admins can update Slack mappings. The UI always shows the current mapping state before you save.
slackIntegration.fetchErrorTitle: Slack connected, but channels could not be loaded
slackIntegration.fetchErrorBody: Reconnect Slack or refresh after bot/channel permissions are fixed.
slackIntegration.disconnectedBody: The Slack workspace summary and channel list appear automatically after OAuth completes. Existing mappings stay visible once a connection is restored.
slackIntegration.emptyBody: We could not find any visible Slack channels for this workspace. Check channel access or reconnect Slack and try again.
slackIntegration.mappedElsewhereHint: This channel currently belongs to {project}. Saving here will trigger a remap confirmation.
slackIntegration.saveFailedTitle: Could not save the Slack mapping
slackIntegration.saveFailed: The Slack mapping could not be saved. Please try again.
slackIntegration.loadFailed: The Slack settings panel could not be loaded. Refresh and try again.
slackIntegration.stickyBarBody: Review the updated project assignments, then save them together.
slackIntegration.remapDialogBody: {channel} is already mapped to {project}. Continue only if you want to move the channel to another project.
slackIntegration.remapDialogWarning: Remapping takes effect immediately for future syncs and outbound notifications.

byomKeys.title: BYOM Key Management
byomKeys.description: Bring your own model key. Validate and save a provider API key for this project.
byomKeys.apiKeyUpdatePlaceholder: Enter new key (leave blank to keep existing)
byomKeys.baseUrlHint: Required for OpenAI-compatible providers and must include /v1.
byomKeys.validationSuccess: API key is valid and working.
byomKeys.validationError: API key validation failed. Check the key and try again.
byomKeys.deleteSuccessWithRotation: Saved BYOM key deleted. KMS rotation request recorded.
byomKeys.deleteConfirmDesc: This will remove the saved API key from this project. AI features that depend on it will stop working.

mcpConnections.title: External MCP connections
mcpConnections.description: Connect approved GitHub, Linear, and Jira MCP servers, validate their tool catalog, and request manual review for anything outside the allowlist.
mcpConnections.loadError: Could not load MCP connection settings.
mcpConnections.connectError: Could not validate the MCP connection.
mcpConnections.disconnectError: Could not remove the MCP connection.
mcpConnections.disconnectSuccess: The MCP connection was removed and live deployments were suspended.
mcpConnections.requestError: Could not submit the MCP review request.
mcpConnections.requestSuccess: Review request submitted. Custom MCP servers stay pending until manual approval.
mcpConnections.githubOAuthError: GitHub MCP connection failed. Retry the OAuth flow.
mcpConnections.customRequestDescription: Servers outside the approved allowlist require manual review before they can be connected.
mcpConnections.requestNotesPlaceholder: Why this MCP server is needed

landing.badgeText: v2.0: multi-agent orchestration for real sprint teams

hero.headline: From BYOA pilots to agent teams that ship
hero.subheadline: Sprintable gives buyers one clear story: start with your own AI agents, keep delivery visible in the sprint surface, then upgrade to hosted orchestration when the team needs a durable command chain.
hero.noteTitle: What buyers learn in one scroll
hero.noteBody: Value prop, target customer, proof, and pricing all line up before signup.
hero.workflowTitle: A sprint flow buyers can understand
hero.workflowSummary: Plan with humans, execute with agents, keep every handoff readable.
hero.workflowStoryValue: PO sets scope and acceptance criteria in one place
hero.workflowMemoValue: Dev and QA agents reply in-thread instead of vanishing into chat silos
hero.workflowShipValue: Reviews, standups, retros, and docs stay connected to delivery

value.label: Value prop
value.title: Explain the product before you explain the stack
value.subtitle: Landing visitors should know who Sprintable is for, what changes after signup, and why pricing expands from BYOA to premium orchestration.

story.title: AI teammates inside the PM surface
story.desc: Sprintable keeps board, memo, standup, retro, and docs in one operator surface so buyers see delivery, not disconnected AI demos.

clarity.title: One upgrade path instead of product confusion
clarity.desc: Free gets teams in with BYOA. Team unlocks agent serving, routing, and shared orchestration when ad-hoc prompting stops scaling.

proof.title: Proof that follows the workflow
proof.desc: Show the command chain, review loop, and pricing in the same scroll so visitors understand what shipping with agents actually looks like.

customers.label: Target customers
customers.title: Who Sprintable is for
customers.subtitle: Built for teams that already feel the pain of AI work living outside the sprint.

founders.title: Founder-operators
founders.desc: You need one surface to plan, delegate, and ship without building a custom internal stack first.
founders.fit: Best when you want BYOA to start now and a paid path when orchestration becomes repeatable.

product.title: AI-native product teams
product.desc: You need humans and agents in the same command chain, not across five dashboards and chat threads.
product.fit: Best when PM, Dev, and QA all need shared visibility into the same delivery loop.

services.title: Studios, agencies, and enablement teams
services.desc: You need reusable workflows, clearer pricing, and a client-safe story for how agents participate in delivery.
services.fit: Best when you need to prove process, not just prompts.

model.label: Operating model
model.title: Choose your AI operating model
model.subtitle: Explain BYOA and agent serving like a buying decision, not an infrastructure lecture.
model.bridgeTitle: Start lean, upgrade when orchestration is worth paying for
model.bridgeBody: BYOA is the low-friction entry. Agent serving is for always-on workflows, shared routing, and premium automation inside the team loop.

byoa.title: BYOA
byoa.desc: Bring your own agents and connect Sprintable to the models and tools you already trust.
bullets: Great for pilots, founders, and technical teams validating the workflow
Includes MCP-native connection points and a free entry path
Best when one or two agents are enough and you control the runtime

serving.title: Agent serving
serving.desc: Use Sprintable-managed orchestration when you need a durable PM → Dev → QA command chain.
bullets: Built for shared team workflows, routing, reviews, and always-on execution
Turns agent participation into a product capability instead of a manual setup
Best when multiple teammates depend on the same orchestration layer

proof.label: Proof section
proof.title: Proof, not platform theater
proof.subtitle: Show how work moves from idea to done with humans and agents in the same loop.

step1.title: Create the story once
step1.desc: The landing promise matches the product: board, memo, acceptance criteria, and owner context stay together from the first click.

step2.title: Keep agent handoffs readable
step2.desc: Agents reply in-thread, humans stay in control, and delivery updates are visible without leaving the sprint surface.

step3.title: Make QA part of the demo
step3.desc: Visitors can see that Sprintable is not just generation. It includes review loops, retry paths, and merge-ready handoffs.

step4.title: Upgrade only when the workflow deserves it
step4.desc: The pricing story lands because the product story lands first: BYOA for entry, agent serving for repeatable team execution.

pricing.title: Pricing that grows with your workflow
pricing.subtitle: Start with BYOA, pay when shared orchestration becomes operationally valuable.

team.name: Team
team.audience: For teams running shared agent workflows

pro.name: Enterprise
pro.audience: For orgs that need governance and rollout support

landing.pricingFootnote: BYOA gets you started. Agent serving is the step-up when routing, shared ownership, and durable automation matter.

finalCta.label: CTA
finalCta.title: Give visitors one clean answer: start free now, upgrade when the team is ready
finalCta.desc: The landing page should let buyers self-qualify in minutes, then go straight to signup, pricing, or docs.

landing.footerDesc: Open-source PM and agent operations surface for teams that want delivery proof, not AI hype.

shell.projectSelectDescription: Choose the current project from the header switcher to activate this surface.
shell.searchPlaceholder: Search tasks, docs, and operator surfaces...

standup.title: Daily Standup
standup.surfaceDescription: Update your standup and review the team state on one operator surface.
standup.myStandupDescription: Capture what you did, what you will do, and what is blocked.
standup.teamDescription: Review team standups for the selected date.
standup.currentSprintDescription: Track the current sprint stories together with the standup surface.
standup.noActiveSprintDescription: Activate a sprint to connect standups to current story context.
standup.noSprintStoriesDescription: This sprint does not have any linked stories yet.
standup.linkedStoriesDescription: Choose the sprint stories that belong in this standup.
standup.noLinkedStoriesDescription: Link sprint stories to keep this entry connected to the current sprint.
standup.peopleDescription: Human teammates and their current standups.
standup.agentsDescription: Automated teammates and their current standups.
standup.noMembersDescription: Add team members to start the standup surface.
standup.teamReviewDescription: Review the current entry and feedback.
standup.noEntryForMemberDescription: This teammate has not posted a standup for the selected date.
standup.yourEntryPending: Your standup entry is still empty.
standup.feedbackDescription: Leave review comments or feedback on this standup.
standup.actionFailed: Something went wrong. Please try again.
standup.loadFailedDescription: Try again or refresh the page.

retro.surfaceDescription: Create retro sessions and monitor their current phase from one operator surface.

docs.noPolicyEpics: No epic specs for this sprint
docs.noSourceMemos: No source memos found for this document.

docs.statusConflict: Conflict: another user edited this document

pricing.downgradeWarningDesc: Downgrading may limit some features. Existing resources will be kept but you cannot create new ones beyond the plan limit.

billing.title: Billing
billing.grandfathered: You are on a grandfathered plan with price guarantee.
billing.upgradeCtaDesc: Upgrade to Team or Pro to unlock more features.

billing.cancelModalDesc: Choose how you want to cancel.
billing.cancelEndOfPeriod: Cancel at end of billing period

usage.title: Usage
usage.surfaceDescription: Review monthly usage, slice it by project or operator view, export the current slice, and verify threshold alerts from one operator surface.
usage.querySurfaceBody: Filter by month and project, then inspect the current cost, token, and runtime breakdown before taking action.
usage.costHint: Computed run cost across the selected slice
usage.exportBody: Download the currently selected month, project filter, and breakdown grouping as CSV for sharing or offline review.
usage.alertBody: Track the monthly cap, threshold trigger, and recently emitted billing alerts without leaving the operator surface.
usage.alertScopeAllProjectsBody: This section reflects billing limits and alert history across every project in the organization.
usage.alertScopeSelectedProjectBody: The selected project "{project}" only changes the query table and CSV export above. This budget alert section stays organization-wide.
usage.alertScopeHint: Alert scope: organization-wide billing limits and alert history
usage.noAlerts: No billing alerts recorded for this month.
usage.noUsage: No usage data for this month

agents.deployDescription: Use the Stitch-informed wizard to launch an agent for {project}. The flow stays scoped to the current project unless you widen it explicitly.
agents.personaStepTitle: Choose the persona that should drive this deployment
agents.personaStepBody: Built-in personas are ready immediately. Custom personas can be linked in from the same deployment flow.
agents.emptyPersonaGroup: No personas in this group yet.
agents.personaDescriptionFallback: Persona description is not available yet.
agents.modelStepTitle: Choose how the deployment should run its model access
agents.modelStepBody: Managed uses Sprintable-managed credentials. BYOM reuses the current project's encrypted AI settings instead of pasting a launch-only secret into the deployment contract.
agents.modeManagedBody: Use Sprintable-managed credentials with an explicit provider and model contract.
agents.modeByomBody: Reuse the current project's encrypted BYOM credential for this deployment.
agents.managedHint: Managed mode uses the shared deployment contract only, without persisting inline secrets on the deployment row.
agents.byomHintWithDefaults: BYOM mode will reuse the current project's encrypted {provider} credential for this deployment.
agents.byomProviderLockedHint: BYOM provider is locked to the current project's AI settings provider: {provider}.
agents.byomHintWithoutDefaults: BYOM mode needs a project AI credential first. Configure AI settings before continuing.
agents.scopeStepTitle: Choose where this deployment can operate
agents.scopeStepBody: Keep it inside the current project, widen to specific projects, or fan out to the whole organization.
agents.scopeAllProjectsBody: The deployment can work across every project in this organization.
agents.scopeSpecificProjectsBody: Choose an explicit list of projects for tighter rollout control.
agents.scopeAllProjectsHint: This will grant access across {count} project(s) in the organization.
agents.reviewStepBody: Double-check the persona, model path, and scope. The launch button stays disabled until each step is complete.
agents.autoRoutingPreviewBody: The wizard previews the routing rules that will be applied after this deployment joins the current project lane.
agents.autoRoutingPreviewNoneBody: This persona mix does not create automatic routing rules yet. The project keeps its current routing setup.
agents.autoRoutingPreviewSoloBody: Solo developer deployments do not create routing rules automatically. The deployed agent keeps handling work directly.
agents.autoRoutingOverwriteWarning: {count} existing routing rule(s) already exist in this project. The automatic template will replace that workflow snapshot only after you confirm the overwrite.
agents.autoRoutingOverwriteConfirm: I understand and want to replace the existing routing rules with this automatic template.
agents.deployingBody: Deployment is in progress. Current status: {status}.
agents.deploySuccessToastBody: {name} is active and ready in the status dashboard.
agents.deployFailedBody: The deployment moved into DEPLOY_FAILED. Review the runtime state and try again.
agents.deployFailedHint: This modal is designed to open when the backend reports DEPLOY_FAILED during polling.
agents.deployStatusLoadFailed: The deployment status could not be refreshed.
agents.noAgentTitle: No active agent record is available for this project
agents.noAgentBody: Create or seed an active agent team member first, then return to the deployment wizard.
agents.emptyPersonasBody: Create at least one built-in or custom persona before launching a deployment from this wizard.
agents.statusDescription: Track launch status, model choice, and persona usage without leaving the operator shell.
agents.statusListBody: Live agent cards with status, execution summary, and today's token usage.
agents.healthSummaryBody: Separate the current deployment state from the recovery actions operators may need next.
agents.healthStateBody_healthy: No active failure or operator intervention is currently blocking this deployment.
agents.healthStateBody_recovering: A recent runtime failure is already moving through the retry path, so the deployment is in recovery mode.
agents.healthStateBody_attention: A recent failure, HITL queue, or deployment problem is still active and needs operator follow-up.
agents.healthStateBody_paused: This deployment is intentionally suspended and queued work will stay held until you resume it.
agents.healthStateBody_deploying: The deployment is still activating, so the runtime health signal is not final yet.
agents.recentFailureEmpty: No active failure signal is attached to this deployment right now.
agents.recoveryCuesBody: These are the operator actions to take next when the deployment is blocked or recovering.
agents.recoveryNoneBody: The deployment is serving normally and there is no active operator follow-up item on this card.
agents.recoveryCueBody_hitl: Resolve the pending HITL request before the response deadline expires.
agents.recoveryCueBody_deploy_failed: The deployment itself failed. Re-open the deploy flow and verify runtime readiness before retrying.
agents.recoveryCueBody_resume_deployment: This deployment is paused. Resume it to release held runs back into the execution queue.
agents.recoveryCueBody_retrying: A failed run already entered the retry path. Watch run history and the trigger memo before intervening.
agents.recoveryCueBody_manual_retry: Automatic retries are exhausted or unavailable. Inspect the failed run, then relaunch manually if it is still safe.
agents.recoveryCueBody_inspect_failure: A recent failure is recorded without an active retry path. Inspect the run and memo thread before changing deployment state.
agents.emptyDeploymentsBody: Launch your first agent deployment from the wizard.
agents.suspendDialogBody: Suspending will hold all queued runs until the deployment is resumed. Active executions will finish normally.
agents.resumeDialogBody: Resuming will reactivate the deployment and release any held runs back into the execution queue.
agents.transitionFailedBody: Could not update the deployment status. Please try again.
agents.personaComposerDescription: Compose a deployable persona from a base persona, custom prompts, safety-layer visibility, token preview, and explicit tool allowlists.
agents.personaComposerSectionBody: Start from a base persona, then layer your own system prompt, behavior rules, and optional tool overrides before creating the persona record.
agents.personaComposerBody: Create the custom persona through POST /api/v1/agent-personas for now, then return here to deploy it once the persona record exists.
agents.personaComposerDescriptionPlaceholder: Summarize the role, tone, and expected working style for this persona.
agents.personaComposerSystemPromptPlaceholder: Describe the operating instructions, priorities, and context that should be merged on top of the base persona.
agents.personaComposerBehaviorRulesPlaceholder: Optional style and behavior guidance. Example: prefer concise replies, cite sources, and escalate blockers immediately.
agents.personaComposerToolsBody: Keep the base persona's defaults or switch to an explicit allowlist for built-in tools and configured MCP tools.
agents.personaComposerToolsInheritedHelp: No override will be saved. The created persona inherits the effective allowlist from the selected base persona.
agents.personaComposerBuiltInGroupHint: Built-in Sprintable actions available to the runtime.
agents.personaComposerMcpGroupHint: Project-configured MCP tools exposed through the current AI settings.
agents.personaComposerNoMcpTools: No MCP tools are configured for this project yet. Built-in tools can still be allowlisted here.
agents.personaComposerSubmitBody: This creates the persona immediately in the current project so it can be selected in the deploy wizard.
agents.personaComposerCreateError: Could not create the custom persona. Please review the form and try again.
agents.personaComposerSuccessBody: Saved as slug `{slug}`. You can return to the deploy wizard and assign it to a deployment now.
agents.personaComposerPreviewBody: Inspect the selected base persona, the managed safety layer, and an estimated prompt footprint before saving.
agents.personaComposerResolvedPromptEmpty: Add prompt content to preview the merged runtime prompt.
agents.personaComposerNoAgentsDescription: Activate an agent in the current project first, then return here to create a custom persona for it.
agents.personaComposerBoundaryBody: This boundary is resolved from the persona allowlist, approved project MCP tools, and the runtime tool registry.
agents.personaComposerBoundaryServersEmpty: No external MCP servers are in scope for this persona.
agents.personaComposerHistoryBody: Recent publish and delete events are persisted in agent audit logs so operators can trace the current rollback target.
agents.personaComposerHistoryEmpty: No persona change history has been recorded yet.
agents.workflowEditorDescription: Design routing flows for {project} with drag-and-drop nodes, edge rules, and reusable templates.
agents.workflowRuleCount: {count, plural, =0 {No rules} one {# rule} other {# rules}}
agents.workflowTemplatesBody: Load a starter workflow, then adjust nodes and rule priorities.
agents.workflowMembersBody: Drag a member onto the canvas or click to place them.
agents.workflowNoHumans: No human members are active in this project yet.
agents.workflowDragHint: Drag from this panel to the canvas. Human targets map back to the original assignee when saved.
agents.workflowCanvasBody: Start an edge from an agent node, click the next node, then tune memo types and the routing action.
agents.workflowEmptyBody: Drop an agent or person onto the canvas, then connect nodes to define the routing flow.
agents.workflowConnectMode: Connection mode is on. Click the next node to create an edge.
agents.workflowPriorityBody: The list order becomes `priority ASC` when the workflow is saved.
agents.workflowPriorityEmpty: No edges yet. Create a connection to define the first routing rule.
agents.workflowEdgeConfigBody: Adjust memo types and the routing action for the selected edge.
agents.workflowNoEdgeSelected: Select an edge on the canvas or in the priority list to edit it.
agents.workflowOriginalAssigneeHint: Fallback target used for report/original-owner return paths.
agents.workflowForwardAgentHint: Forward edges keep the next agent as `forward_to_agent_id`.
agents.workflowHumanTargetHint: Human targets serialize back to the original assignee so S523 forward/report semantics stay intact.
agents.workflowCycleWarningBody: This workflow contains a routing cycle: {cycles}. Saving is still allowed, but review the path carefully.
agents.workflowSaveSuccessBody: The canvas was serialized into agent_routing_rules and saved successfully.
agents.workflowSaveErrorBody: The workflow could not be saved.
agents.workflowNodeAlreadyPlacedBody: {name} is already on the canvas, so the existing node was reused.
agents.workflowHumanSourceBody: Only agent nodes can start saved routing edges. Use people as readable endpoints instead.
agents.workflowSelfLoopBody: This edge loops back to the same node. A warning banner will stay visible, but saving is allowed.
agents.workflowEdgeCreatedBody: Update the selected edge in the popover to finish the rule.
agents.workflowEdgeDeletedBody: The selected workflow edge was removed.
agents.workflowMobileBody: Review the current routing order on mobile, then switch to a desktop-sized screen to edit the canvas.
agents.workflowDesktopHint: Desktop editing is available on PC screens. Mobile keeps a readable summary only.
agents.workflowNoAgentsBody: Add or activate an agent in this project first, then return to configure workflow routing.
agents.orchestrationGateDescription: Agent deployments, custom personas, and workflow routing are available on the Team plan and above. Upgrade to unlock the full orchestration suite.
agents.verifyStepBody: Complete and record post-deploy verification before treating the deployment flow as done.
agents.deployPreflightBody: Validate blocking deployment issues before creating the managed deployment.
agents.deployPreflightRoutingValue: {template} · {count, plural, =0 {No rules} one {# rule} other {# rules}}
agents.deployPreflightReadyBody: Preflight passed. You can start the deployment now.
agents.deployPreflightBlockedBody: Resolve these blocking issues before deploying.
agents.deployPreflightPendingBody: Run preflight to separate setup problems from actual deployment failures.
agents.deployPreflightFailedBody: Unable to load deployment preflight results.
agents.verificationChecklistBody: Use these checkpoints to confirm the deployment is ready for real work.
agents.verificationCheckpointDashboard: Open the Agents dashboard and confirm the deployment shows as ACTIVE.
agents.verificationCheckpointRouting: Review the workflow editor and confirm the expected routing preview ({count, plural, =0 {no rules} one {# rule} other {# rules}}).
agents.verificationCheckpointMcp: Confirm MCP validation stayed clean after preflight (current error count: {count}).
agents.verificationCheckpointMcpFallback: Confirm external integrations and MCP connections are ready for the deployment.
agents.verificationPendingBody: {name} is active, but post-deploy verification has not been recorded yet.
agents.verificationCompletedBody: {name} is active and the post-deploy verification result has been recorded.
agents.verificationPendingHint: Review the checklist below, then record completion from this step.
agents.verificationCompleteSuccessBody: Post-deploy verification has been recorded for {name}.
agents.verificationCompleteFailedBody: Unable to persist the post-deploy verification result.
agents.workflowDryRunBody: Preview a memo lane before rollout and verify the real routing handoff.
agents.workflowDryRunNoRule: No rule matches. The original assignee keeps the memo.
agents.workflowExpectedPathsBody: Compare live and draft paths so rollout impact stays explicit.
agents.workflowRolloutChecklistBody: Confirm dry-run, path review, and recovery readiness before saving live rules.
agents.workflowRolloutChangedRules: {count, plural, =0 {No changed rules} one {# changed rule} other {# changed rules}}
agents.workflowRolloutAddedRules: {count, plural, =0 {No added rules} one {# added rule} other {# added rules}}
agents.workflowRolloutRemovedRules: {count, plural, =0 {No removed rules} one {# removed rule} other {# removed rules}}
agents.workflowRolloutNoChanges: No draft changes yet. Edit the canvas to unlock rollout review.
agents.workflowRolloutChecklistDryRun: I reviewed the dry-run simulation for the memo types affected by this rollout.
agents.workflowRolloutChecklistExpectedPaths: I compared live and draft expected paths and confirmed the changed memo lanes.
agents.workflowRolloutChecklistRecovery: I confirmed the rollback or disable path before shipping this routing change.
agents.workflowRolloutRollbackReady: Rollback snapshot is ready. You can restore the previous live workflow immediately if the rollout regresses.
agents.workflowRolloutRollbackMissing: No previous live snapshot is available yet. First rollout only has the disable-all guardrail.
agents.workflowRolloutDisableReady: Disable-all is ready. If the rollout misroutes live memos, you can fail back to the original assignee quickly.
agents.workflowRolloutDisableEmpty: There are no live routing rules yet. Disable-all will become available after the first rollout.
agents.workflowDraftInvalidBody: Draft preview is blocked until the canvas is valid: {error}
agents.workflowEmergencyBody: Keep rollback and disable separate from dry-run review so live recovery stays fast under pressure.
agents.workflowRollbackSuccessBody: The previous live routing snapshot was restored.
agents.workflowRollbackErrorBody: The previous live snapshot could not be restored.
agents.workflowDisableSuccessBody: All live routing rules were disabled in place.
agents.workflowDisableErrorBody: Live routing rules could not be disabled.

agentRuns.title: Agent run history
agentRuns.description: Browse agent execution records, filter by status and date, and inspect individual run timelines.
agentRuns.emptyDescription: No runs match the current filters. Adjust the date range or status filter.
agentRuns.noTimelineEntries: No timeline entries recorded for this run.
agentRuns.noToolAuditEntries: No tool audit entries recorded for this run.
agentRuns.memoryCompactionThresholds: Thresholds: importance >= {minImportance}, stale cleanup after {maxAgeDays} days when importance < 50, duplicate similarity cutoff {duplicateSimilarity}.
agentRuns.retrySuccessBody: A new run has been queued for this agent.
agentRuns.retryFailedBody: Could not schedule a retry. Please try again later.
agentRuns.runHistoryDescription: View past agent executions and their outcomes.

agentHitl.title: HITL approval requests
agentHitl.description: Review pending human-in-the-loop approvals and adjust the project HITL policy.
agentHitl.sectionBody: Requests stay here until an admin resolves the linked HITL memo.
agentHitl.emptyDescription: Once an agent asks for approval, the request and linked memo will appear here.
agentHitl.policyBody: Tune the high-risk catalog, approval routing, and timeout classes for this project.
agentHitl.policyLoadFailed: Failed to load the HITL policy
agentHitl.policySaveFailed: Failed to save the HITL policy
agentHitl.catalogBody: Read-only operator view of the action classes that should steer the model toward HITL.
agentHitl.catalog_destructive_change_body: Memo/story resolution, deletion, and other irreversible state changes.
agentHitl.catalog_external_side_effect_body: Writes to external systems, public channels, or third-party tools.
agentHitl.catalog_credential_or_billing_change_bod: Credential rotation, billing-impacting actions, or cost override flows.
agentHitl.approvalBody: Each operator-controlled HITL event uses the approval contract, while timeout class remains configurable.
agentHitl.approval_manual_hitl_request_body: Default rule used when the model explicitly asks for human judgment.
agentHitl.approval_billing_cap_exceeded_body: Policy for per-run cap overruns before the operator allows further action.
agentHitl.timeoutBody: Define reminder lead time, total deadline, and timeout escalation handling.
agentHitl.timeout_fast_body: For urgent approvals and time-sensitive cost or permission decisions.
agentHitl.timeout_standard_body: Default SLA for normal human review requests.
agentHitl.timeout_extended_body: For requests that can wait longer or cross non-working hours.
agentHitl.escalationMode_timeout_memo_and_escalate: Create timeout memo and escalate to another admin
agentHitl.policySummaryBody: This exact summary is injected into the agent system prompt.
